t_ch: 再见。
t_en: Bye.

t_ch: 欢迎下次光临！
t_en: Come back next time!

t_ch: 哟，你哪位？这儿是博物馆修理间，可没展览给你看哪……
t_en: Hey, who are you? This is the restoration room of the museum, nothing here is on display...

t_ch: 我是佩拉小姐派来帮忙的。
t_en: Pela sent me to help.

t_ch: 佩拉小姐派来帮忙的……
t_en: Miss Pela sent you to help...

t_ch: 我是贝洛伯格的大英雄。
t_en: I'm the hero of Belobog.

t_ch: 贝洛伯格的大英雄……
t_en: The hero of Belobog...

t_ch: 我是{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I'm {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}……
t_en: {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: 哎哟，我想起来了——哈哈，你瞧我这记性！
t_en: Oh, now I remember! Haha, my forgetfulness!

t_ch: 幸会幸会，我是博物馆的文物修复员鲍里斯！刚才没能认出来，实在是不好意思啊。
t_en: Pleased to meet you, I'm the museum's artifact restorer, Boris! So sorry for not recognizing you earlier.

t_ch: 哟，你怎么又逛荡到修理间来了？
t_en: Hey, why'd you come back to the restoration room?

t_ch: 难不成…你是找我来聊天的？
t_en: Are you... looking to have a chat with me?

t_ch: 想和你聊聊天…
t_en: I want to chat with you...

t_ch: 哈哈，难得啊，有人会来找我这修文物的聊闲天——你想聊点啥？我都可以奉陪！
t_en: Hah hah, that's rare, someone wanting to chat with me, an artifact restorer! What would you like to talk about? I'm down for anything!

t_ch: 博物馆是做什么的？
t_en: What does the museum do?

t_ch: 博物馆嘛，自然就是展览各种文物的地方了。
t_en: Well, it's a museum, so naturally, it puts up all kinds of relics and artifacts on display.

t_ch: 至于我们这座历史文化博物馆嘛，展品谈不上多——原因你也知道，寒潮、裂界…它们带走的东西实在是太多了。
t_en: This museum in particular doesn't have much in its collection, and you know the reason for it — the Eternal Freeze, the Fragmentum... They took so much from us.

t_ch: 不过，我们还是把能收集来的文物全都翻新了一遍，又把它们送进展柜里。也正因此，这座博物馆看起来还算是过得去。
t_en: However, we still collected everything we can and restored them so that we can put them on display. Because of that, the museum is at least somewhat decent.

t_ch: 总之…如果你在参观过程中觉得有些不满，还请你多见谅——这已经是我们能尽到的最大的努力了。
t_en: Anyway... please understand — if you feel dissatisfied during the tour — this is the best that we can do.

t_ch: 你的工作是什么？
t_en: What does your job entail?

t_ch: 文物修复员嘛，那工作自然就是修复各种文物了。你现在能在展馆里看到的各种文物和比例模型，基本都出自我的手。
t_en: As an artifact restorer, my job is naturally to restore various cultural and historical pieces. Most of the artifacts and scale models that you can see in the exhibition hall were basically restored by me.

t_ch: 说起来，我当年还是贝洛伯格艺术学院雕塑系的优秀毕业生呢——于尔丹喊我来帮忙制作模型，没想到竟然也把修复文物的活计交给我了……
t_en: Speaking of which, I was once an outstanding graduate from the Belobog College of Arts back then, specializing in sculptures. Uldan hired me to help create models, and I ended up having a job here as a restorer...

t_ch: 哎，贝洛伯格的专业人才还是太少了啊。
t_en: *sigh* There are still too few professionals in Belobog.

t_ch: 我没问题了，就到这里吧。
t_en: I have no further questions.

t_ch: 慢走！好好享受我们的展览吧。
t_en: Take care! Enjoy our exhibition.

t_ch: 我先走了。
t_en: I'm heading off, then.

t_ch: 慢走！好好享受我们的展览吧。
t_en: Take care! Enjoy our exhibition.

t_ch: 欢迎来到贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆！请问有什么可以帮到您吗？
t_en: Welcome to the Belobog History and Culture Museum. Is there anything I can help you with?

t_ch: 想问一些事…
t_en: I have some questions...

t_ch: 您是在游览过程中遇到什么麻烦了吗？我可以为您一一解答！
t_en: Did you run into any trouble during your tour? I can try to answer them all for you!

t_ch: 博物馆是做什么的？
t_en: What does the museum do?

t_ch: 贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆是克里珀堡文化部辖下的、全贝洛伯格最大的综合性博物馆，内容涵盖了历史文化与艺术等不同范畴！
t_en: Belobog History and Culture Museum is the largest comprehensive museum in Belobog under the Ministry of Culture in Qlipoth, covering a wide range of history, culture and art!

t_ch: 我们博物馆立志于给每一位来访观众提供最温馨便捷的观展体验。在您参观文物之余，也不妨在休息区小憩一番，或是参加我们不定期举行的主题活动节目！
t_en: Our museum is dedicated to providing a welcoming and accessible experience for all visitors. While exploring the exhibits, be sure to take a break in our rest area. And don't miss out on our occasional themed events!

t_ch: 博物馆里都有什么？
t_en: What do you have in your exhibitions?

t_ch: 贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆现共设有三个常设展览馆，包括：综合展馆、工业展馆以及历史文化展馆！
t_en: There are three permanent exhibition areas in the Belobog History and Culture Museum, and they are: The General Hall, the Industrial Hall, and the History-Culture Hall!

t_ch: 不同展馆中设有不同种类的历史文物，您可以在此欣赏到贝洛伯格历史文化的不同侧面；此外，我们还将不定期举办专题展览，您也可以随时前来参加！
t_en: The three different areas each have different kinds of historical artifacts on display. You can see Belobog from different angles in each of the areas. Other than that, we will occasionally have themed exhibitions that you can check out!

t_ch: 想了解艾丽斯小姐…
t_en: I want to know more about you, Eris...

t_ch: 哎？！我吗……
t_en: Eh!? Me...?

t_ch: 我、我只是个贝洛伯格艺术学院的学生而已啦…馆长于尔丹先生是我的导师，我跟着先生来这里开展实习……
t_en: I... I'm just a student of the Belobog College of Art... Curator Uldan is my mentor, and I'm here to do my internship under him...

t_ch: 总、总之，我只是个平平无奇的普通人而已，没经历过什么值得一听的故事……
t_en: I—In short, I'm just an ordinary person, and my stories are as boring as they come...

t_ch: 我、我们还是来聊聊博物馆本身吧？！
t_en: L—Let's talk about museum, shall we?

t_ch: 我想了解的就是这些。
t_en: That's all I wanted to know.

t_ch: 请慢走，也祝您享受本次博物馆之旅！
t_en: Please take care, and hope you enjoy the tour!

t_ch: 不打扰你了。
t_en: I'll leave you to it.

t_ch: 请慢走，也祝您享受本次博物馆之旅！
t_en: Please take care, and hope you enjoy the tour!

t_ch: 欢迎来到贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Welcome to the Belobog History and Culture Museum, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 多亏了你，我们的展览才能像这样正常举行——再次感谢你为贝洛伯格的贡献。
t_en: Thanks to you, our exhibition is successfully pulled off. We thank you for your service to Belobog once again.

t_ch: 啊…是{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}！欢迎来到博物馆……
t_en: Ah! It's {F#big sister}{M#big brother} {NICKNAME}! Welcome to the museum...

t_ch: …对不起，这里应该说全称——欢迎来到贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆！请随意参观哦，{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}！
t_en: ...Sorry, I should say its full name... Welcome to the Belobog History and Culture Museum! Take your time around here, {F#big sister}{M#big brother} {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我们又见面了。如你所见，我正在担任志愿导览员……
t_en: We meet again, {NICKNAME}. As you can see, I'm currently volunteering as a tour guide...

t_ch: 在最开始的时候，观众们都以为我是来视察展览状况的，根本不敢上前询问…不过，现在大家似乎已经渐渐习惯了——不得不说，这可真是新奇的体验。
t_en: At first, visitors thought I was here to monitor the status of the exhibition. They were scared to approach me... But now, it seems like everyone started getting used to it — you know, this is a novel experience.

t_ch: 哈，这不是我的老主顾、兼贝洛伯格的名侦探{NICKNAME}嘛！你也来参观展览啊，真是好兴致！
t_en: Ah, isn't this my old pal, the famed detective of Belobog, {NICKNAME}? You're here to visit the exhibition as well? What an adventure!

t_ch: 不瞒你说，我现在正在这儿担任志愿导览员呢——当然，也顺便逗逗那位情报官小姑娘——就让我看看能在她眼皮子底下藏到什么时候吧，嘿嘿。
t_en: To be honest, I'm currently volunteering as a tour guide here. Also, I'm teasing the intelligence officer lady over there... Let's see how long I can hide while within her purview, teehee.

t_ch: 哟，这不是{NICKNAME}嘛。怎么，你也是来找我要签名的？
t_en: Hey, isn't this {NICKNAME}? Why, are you here to get my signature as well?

t_ch: 嗯？你说志愿导览员？你放心，我可没忘记这茬——这不是大家见了我都纷纷热情高涨，我实在招架不住嘛…哎，我也很为难的啦～
t_en: Hm? Volunteer tour guide? Don't worry, I didn't forget. It's just everyone was so excited, I can't hold on any longer... it's difficult holding back the passion, y'know~

t_ch: 又见面了，{NICKNAME}。能在诊所之外碰到你，还怪让人不习惯的呢。
t_en: We meet again, {NICKNAME}. Meeting you outside of the context of my clinic is a bit hard to get used to.

t_ch: 随意参观吧，如果想了解展品背后的故事也不妨告诉我——身为儿童公社的负责人，我可是很擅长讲故事的哦。
t_en: Just take a look around. If you want to find out the stories behind each exhibit, you can come to me. As the person in charge of the clinic nursery, I'm pretty good at telling stories.

t_ch: 哇，是荣誉队员{NICKNAME}！你在博物馆里有没有看到坏人呀？
t_en: Wow, it's our honorary member {NICKNAME}! Did you see any bad guys in the museum?

t_ch: 如果有的话，一定要立刻来告诉虎克哦！为了让大家好好参观，虎克一定会和洞洞机一起把坏人赶跑的！
t_en: If you have, you must tell Hook immediately! Hook and Diggertron will chase the bad guys away to make sure the museum visitors have a good time!

t_ch: 欢迎来到贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆，{NICKNAME}。我是博物馆的志愿导览员，兼银鬃铁卫戍卫官，杰帕德。
t_en: Welcome to the Belobog History and Culture Museum, {NICKNAME}. I am a volunteer tour guide of the museum and the Silvermane Guard Captain, Gepard.

t_ch: 如果你有任何问题，都可以向我提问。愿您能有一段美好的观展体验。
t_en: If you have any questions, feel free to ask me about it. I hope you will have a wonderful exhibit experience.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，欢迎来到贝洛伯格历史文化……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, welcome to the Belobog History and Cul—

t_ch: 咳咳。虽然排练了好多遍，但这还是太尴尬了…我看，咱俩之间就没必要端着了吧？
t_en: Ahem. It still feels too awkward even after so many rehearsals... I think we can skip the formalities, right?

t_ch: 欢迎来到贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆，外来者。
t_en: Welcome to the Belobog History and Culture Museum, outsider.

t_ch: 在克拉拉的请求下，我来到此处为她提供协助。因此，如有必要，我将为你提供路线指引及展品解说服务。
t_en: I am here to provide assistance to Clara at her request. Therefore, if there is a need, I will supply you with a route guideline and exhibit explanation service.

t_ch: 外来…外来者…欢迎…迎…迎…迎…
t_en: Outside... outsider... welcome—come—come—come...

t_ch: 「滴」的一声过后，帕金斯宕机了。
t_en: After a beeping sound, Perkins' system is down.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，这不是大英雄{NICKNAME}嘛！没想到能在这儿又遇到你啊！我架不住那几个铁卫小伙子软磨硬泡，非要我来做什么导览员，嗐！
t_en: Ahahaha, well if it isn't our hero, {NICKNAME}? I didn't think I'd see you here! Those Silvermane kiddos were so persistent, so I had to come and be some tour guide, ugh!

t_ch: 不过，闲着也是闲着，我也就干脆来这儿出份力了——有什么要帮忙的，随时喊我！银鬃铁卫老兵奥列格，随时乐意为你效劳！
t_en: But then again, I do have the time, so I might as well help out... If you need anything, just give me a holler! Silvermane Guard veteran Oleg is always ready to help!

t_ch: 咳咳…我记得你，{NICKNAME}。虎克和我提起过你，你就是那个拯救了贝洛伯格的{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}……
t_en: *cough* I remember you, {NICKNAME}. Hook told me about you. You're the {F#girl}{M#boy} who saved Belobog...

t_ch: …欢迎来到贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆。有什么需要的话，就来找我吧。祝你度过愉快的一天。
t_en: ...Welcome to the Belobog History and Culture Museum. If you require any assistance, feel free to ask me. I hope you have a pleasant day.

t_ch: 咳咳…我记得你，{NICKNAME}。先前我和虎克都受了你不少帮助……
t_en: *cough* ...I remember you, {NICKNAME}. Hook and I received a lot of help from you...

t_ch: …欢迎来到贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆。有什么需要的话，就来找我吧——你是我们父女俩的恩人，我一定有求必应。
t_en: ...Welcome to the Belobog History and Culture Museum. If you require any assistance, feel free to ask me. You are our savior, I will do my best to assist you.

t_ch: 喔，这不是外来者{NICKNAME}么！欢迎来到贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆。
t_en: Hey, isn't this {NICKNAME} the Outsider? Welcome to the Belobog History and Culture Museum.

t_ch: 你随意参观，有什么需要帮助的地方就告诉我——另外，这次可千万记得不要触发警报了…哈哈哈！
t_en: Feel free to walk around. If you need any help, lemme know... Also, try not to trigger the alarm this time... hahaha!

t_ch: 请问…你在这博物馆里，可曾看到过我的希露瓦大人？
t_en: Excuse me... Have you seen my Lady Serval anywhere in the museum?

t_ch: 我本以为希露瓦大人一定会来参观博物馆，就报名做了志愿导览员…可到现在我都没见过希露瓦大人！难道她不会来了吗……
t_en: I was thinking, she will definitely visit the museum, so I signed up to be a volunteer tour guide... But I still haven't seen her until now! Is she not coming?

t_ch: 啊…我好像和你见过面——你也是来寻觅希露瓦大人的身影的吗？
t_en: Ah... I think I've seen you before... Are you here to look for Lady Serval's divine countenance too?

t_ch: 我本以为希露瓦大人一定会来参观博物馆，就报名做了志愿导览员…可到现在我都没见过希露瓦大人！难道她不会来了吗……
t_en: I was thinking, she will definitely visit the museum, so I signed up to be a volunteer tour guide... But I still haven't seen her until now! Is she not coming?

t_ch: 你好，欢迎来到贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆。我是志愿导览员莉拉，如果你需要任何帮助，都可以告诉我。
t_en: Good day, welcome to the Belobog History and Culture Museum. I am volunteer tour guide, Lila. If you require any services, please let me know.

t_ch: 她向你报以一个灿烂的微笑，随后将目光挪向别处，小声嘟哝着：「奇怪，这博物馆里为什么找不到任何雪原生物相关的展品呢……」
t_en: She beams a bright grin at you, then shifts her gaze elsewhere while muttering, "Weird, why can I not find anything about Snow Plains creatures anywhere in this museum..."

t_ch: 欢迎来到贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆！在这里，您将有机会踏寻贝洛伯格的历史足迹，看到世纪奇书《雪国冒险奇谭》中的种种绮丽幻想变为现实……
t_en: Welcome to the Belobog History and Culture Museum! In here, you will have a chance to step on the historical footfalls of Belobog and see the descriptions of the phantasmal scenes from the epic book of the century, Tale of the Winterlands, come to life...

t_ch: 这位《水晶日报》的主编似乎还是忘不了她的《雪国冒险奇谭》……
t_en: Looks like this Crystal Daily chief editor still couldn't let go of her Tale of the Winterlands...

t_ch: 呵，真是悠闲的一天啊。我是博物馆的志愿导览员华劳斯•凯特博瑞屈•莱姆士汀，你需要什么帮助吗？
t_en: Hah, what a leisurely day. I am volunteer tour guide Wallace Cambridge Limestein. Do you require any assistance?

t_ch: 如果想要了解展品背后的故事，尽管来找我——这馆子里的不少展品都是我捐的，要说谁更了解它们，那就必须是我了……
t_en: If you want to know the story behind the exhibits, feel free to approach me. Many of the exhibits in this museum were donated by me. There is no one more familiar with their stories than I do...

t_ch: 喔，这不是荣誉队员{NICKNAME}嘛！你也是来找阿丽…来执行虎克老大的机密任务的吗？
t_en: Hey, honorary member {NICKNAME}! You're also here to look for Ali— to execute Boss Hook's secret mission?

t_ch: 别看我现在是博物馆的志愿导览员，但这都是伪装——你就瞧好了吧，我鼹鼠党的二把手尤利安一定会不辱使命的！
t_en: We may look like your regular volunteer tour guide, but this is just a disguise... Just wait, I, Julian, the second chair of The Moles, will not fail!

t_ch: 呜，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，阿丽娜真的不能收下你的礼物！阿丽娜现在可是志愿导览员……
t_en: *sniffle* {F#Big sister}{M#Big brother}, I can't take your gifts! I'm a volunteer tour guide right now...

t_ch: 咦，原来你不是来送礼物的呀…是遇到什么困难了吗？如果是的话，一定要告诉阿丽娜喔～
t_en: Oh, you're not here to give me anything? Do you need help? Just let me know, okay?

t_ch: 大家都躲到哪里去了…可恶……
t_en: Where did everyone go? Drats...

t_ch: 你确信自己曾经与这位把狡猾写在脸上的小孩子打过照面，但你不确定她有没有意识到自己现在是博物馆的志愿导览员。
t_en: You're sure you've met this little kid who has craftiness written all over her face, but you're not sure if she realizes that you're now a volunteer tour guide at the museum.

t_ch: 你好，我是伊蕾恩，贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆的志愿导览员。如果你需要任何帮助，都可以来告诉我！
t_en: Good day, I am Elaine, a volunteer tour guide of the Belobog History and Culture Museum. If you require any assistance, please let me know.

t_ch: 另外，如果你见过馆长于尔丹的话…可以代我问问他，做这个活真的没有工资吗？拜托了，这对我真的很重要……
t_en: Also, if you've seen our curator Uldan... please ask him about one thing for me. Does this job really not pay? Please, this is important to me...

t_ch: 你好，我是贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆的志愿导览员菲多拉。如果你想了解任何机械知识，都可以来问我哦！
t_en: Good day, I am Fidora, a volunteer tour guide of the Belobog History and Culture Museum. If you want to know about any mechanical know-hows, please let me know.

t_ch: 你有些怀疑她到底有没有搞清楚志愿导览员的工作究竟是什么。
t_en: You're not sure if she even understood what the job scope of a volunteer tour guide entails.

t_ch: 有的垃圾能够得到走进橱窗的机会，而有的垃圾却只能被埋没在漆黑深邃的垃圾桶中，永不见天日……
t_en: Some trash has a chance to be displayed behind a cabinet glass, but some others can only be buried beneath the sunless depths of a trashcan...

t_ch: 而我，到底是哪一种垃圾呢……
t_en: And which type of trash am I...

t_ch: 哔嘟…我是「家用寻物仪『理查德』•版本<unbreak>0.63</unbreak>•专家型•年度豪华版」，昵称「查宝」！
t_en: Beep, beep, beep... I am Richard, the Home-Use Object Finder. Model information: Version <unbreak>0.63</unbreak>, Professional model, Annual Deluxe package. Nickname: "Findie"!

t_ch: 哔嘟…不要把我关进展柜里——我要回星际和平家电门店！我要回星际和平家电门店！
t_en: Beep beep... Do not lock me back in the display case... Must return to Interastral Peace Appliances! Must return to Interastral Peace Appliances!

t_en: What!? I didn't know that, this is a bombshell! Unbelievable, the Architects would spend all the allotted budget on things like this...

t_ch: 这位志愿导览员似乎并不打算来段说唱，还是不要逼她了。

t_en: She's pretty suitable for a job like this.

t_ch: 好久不见，年轻人。祝你享受贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆的探索之旅。

t_ch: 哟，这不是{NICKNAME}嘛！这儿可比咱家那大饭店亮堂多了，你也好好感受一下吧！

t_ch: 这位歌德大饭店的老板娘一如既往地热情。

t_en: Did you see that, Mother?

t_en: Ah, it's you, {NICKNAME}. I was spacing out at my mother's portrait and didn't realize you were here, my apologies.

t_ch: 是我打扰你了。
t_en: I was the one who disturbed you.

t_ch: 呵呵…你还是这么善解人意——也难怪你会帮助于尔丹先生振兴博物馆了。
t_en: Haha... You're still as understanding as ever... It's no wonder you helped Mr. Uldan to revitalize the museum.

t_ch: 你在参观展览吗？
t_en: Are you touring the museum?

t_ch: 呵呵…是啊。
t_en: Oh... yes.

t_ch: 于尔丹先生同我说，在{NICKNAME}的帮助下，这座博物馆终于可以对外完全开放，我便来一探究竟了。
t_en: Mr. Uldan told me that the museum was finally able to fully open to everyone, thanks to you, so I came to check it out.

t_ch: 你看——这座博物馆如今已经找回了属于她的所有藏品，前来欣赏展览的游客摩肩接踵……

t_ch: 这一切都多亏了你，{NICKNAME}——收下吧，{NICKNAME}，这是你应得的。
t_en: This is all thanks to you, {NICKNAME} — Take this, it's what you deserve.

t_ch: 我，贝洛伯格的大守护者，谨代表贝洛伯格和她的全体子民向你表示诚挚的谢意。
t_en: As the Supreme Guardian of Belobog, I would like to express my sincerest gratitude to you on behalf of Belobog and all her people.

t_ch: 收下吧，{NICKNAME}，这是你应得的。

t_en: There are so many exhibits in here!

t_en: Well, you came to the right wispae. Look around! We've got a three-in-one world game collection. Which one catches your eye?

t_en: "The Wispae That Constantly Falls," "The Wispae That Wants To Fly But Can't Fly," and "The Wispae Waiting For Money to Fall From The Sky" — mainly these three fashionable items.

t_ch: …有那种积极的尘灵，肯定也会有这样丧丧的，希望你能喜欢。
t_en: ...Just like there are upbeat wispae, there are bound to be these gloomy ones too. Hope you'll like them.

t_ch: …这里只有我们仨了，你看着办吧。
t_en: ...It's just the three of us here. Your call.

t_ch: …感觉暂时不想玩呢。
t_en: ...I'm not really feeling it right now.

t_en: Drop by again when you're free.

t_en: ...Hm? You can, you can! Meow!

t_ch: 我明白了咪，你是二次元对咪？
t_en: I got it meow, you're a 2D character, aren't you, meow?

t_ch: 你才是二次元咪。
t_en: You're the 2D character... Meow.

t_ch: 对咪，我们是同好咪。
t_en: Yeah, we have the same hobbies, meow.

t_ch: 我确实是二次元咪。
t_en: I am indeed a 2D character, meow.

t_ch: 这位朋友，你看过《破晓战队》吗？你了解《破晓战队》吗？
t_en: Hey friend, have you seen Daybreak Squadron? Are you familiar with Daybreak Squadron?

t_ch: 倒是打过交道…
t_en: We've had some dealings...

t_ch: 听你的语气，就知道你对《破晓战队》的了解尚浅。因为你没有表现出听到这个名字以后应该表现出的狂热。
t_en: I can tell from your tone that you barely know anything about Daybreak Squadron. Because you're not showing the proper level of fanaticism that name should inspire.

t_ch: 但是没关系，现在入坑也不晚。入坑《破晓战队》最好的机会是十年前，其次是现在！
t_en: But that's okay. It's never too late to get into it! The best time to get into Daybreak Squadron was ten years ago. The second best time is now!

t_ch: 这战队的历史都没有十年吧？
t_en: This team hasn't even been around for ten years, right?

t_ch: 没错，十年前的二相乐园没有《破晓战队》，所以十年前的二相乐园还远远不够酷！
t_en: Exactly! Ten years ago, Planarcadia didn't have Daybreak Squadron, so ten years ago, Planarcadia was nowhere near cool enough!

t_ch: 如果你想要入坑《破晓战队》，欢迎随时来问我，我绝对能给你最明确的入坑指南～
t_en: If you ever wanna get into Daybreak Squadron, feel free to hit me up anytime! I can totally give you the ultimate beginner's guide~

t_ch: 如何？想入坑《破晓战队》了吗？
t_en: So? Ready to dive into Daybreak Squadron?

t_ch: 想问你点事。
t_en: I'd like to ask you something.

t_ch: 问吧！只要是和《破晓战队》相关的事，我什么都会回答你的！
t_en: Go ahead, ask away! I'll answer anything and everything about Daybreak Squadron!

t_ch: 你知道破晓战队出了什么事吗？
t_en: Do you know what happened to the Daybreak Squadron?

t_ch: 不就是输给了星穹列车吗？我不在乎。我对《破晓战队》的喜爱，早就不是那种肤浅的喜爱了。
t_en: It lost to the Astral Express, huh? I don't care. My love for Daybreak Squadron has long surpassed that kind of shallow affection.

t_ch: 他们的故事作为一个象征、一个信标，阐明平凡人应该如何成为英雄。
t_en: Their story stands as a symbol, a beacon, showing how ordinary people can become heroes.

t_ch: 而他们所代表过的精神，就算只是演员的临场做戏，也不会被磨损。
t_en: And the spirit they represented, even if it was just actors putting on a show, will never fade.

t_ch: …况且，我原本主要喜欢的也是他们出演的虚构故事啦！
t_en: ...Besides, what I really loved in the first place were the fictional stories they starred in!

t_ch: 有什么入坑推荐吗？
t_en: Got any tips for getting started?

t_ch: 我个人会建议你从他们出演的虚构故事开始看起。因为都是单元剧，先看哪集都可以。我推荐几集最适合入坑的吧。
t_en: Personally, I'd say start with the fictional stories they appear in. Since they're all standalone episodes, you can jump in anywhere. Let me recommend a few that are perfect for newcomers.

t_ch: 首先是《次元怪盗登场》和《次元怪盗归来》，这两集没什么深度，但是视觉奇观非常优秀。次元怪盗先是让世界变成二维，又让世界进入四维，非常精彩。
t_en: First up, we've got The Dimensional Thief Appears and The Dimensional Thief Returns. These two episodes aren't exactly deep, but the visual spectacle is absolutely stellar. The Dimensional Thief first flattens the world into bidimensional, then turns it four-dimensional. Totally mind-blowing stuff.

t_ch: 《不被需要的人们》则正相反，沉郁、阴暗，几乎没什么特效场景。但是故事却非常有深度，反派是一群被新技术淘汰的绘师，看到后面，你会很不希望他们输。
t_en: Those Who Are No Longer Needed, on the other hand, is the complete opposite. Somber, bleak, barely any flashy effects to speak of. But the story's got some real depth. The villains are a bunch of artists who got replaced by new tech, and by the end... you'll be rooting for them not to lose.

t_ch: 最后还有一集《海兽们的长夜》，讲述海洋中诞生的灾异，与破晓战士们坠入爱河的悲剧故事…唉，你还是自己看吧，这个我每次复述剧情都会哭出来。
t_en: Lastly, there's the episode, The Long Night of the Sea Beasts. It tells the tragic story of Aberrations born in the ocean who fall in love with the Daybreak Warriors... *sigh* You know what, just watch it yourself. I cry every single time I try to retell this one.

t_ch: 如果你也喜欢，还可以看一下「海兽前传」三部曲，「湍流」「层流」和「激流」。讲述了海洋灾异为什么会爱上…啊不能再说下去了，鼻头开始酸了。
t_en: If you like it, you should also check out the "Sea Beast" prequel trilogy: "Riptide," "Laminar," and "Torrent." It's about why the Aberrations in the ocean fell in love with... Ah, I can't say anymore, my nose is starting to tingle.

t_ch: 总之先把我推荐的这几集看完吧。看完你就算是正式入坑《破晓战队》了！
t_en: Anyway, start by watching the episodes I recommended. Once you're done with those, you'll officially be a Daybreak Squadron fan!

t_ch: 《破晓战队》里你最喜欢谁？
t_en: Who's your favorite character in Daybreak Squadron?

t_ch: 当然是灰烬。
t_en: Cinderborne, of course.

t_ch: 《破晓战队》的主旨，就是平凡之人所能做到的英雄之事。而在这其中，再没人比灰烬更符合平凡之人的定义了。
t_en: Daybreak Squadron is all about the heroic deeds that ordinary people can accomplish. And among them, no one fits the definition of "ordinary" better than Cinderborne.

t_ch: 我觉得灰烬代表了一种意志——不止天选之子才能成为英雄，你在路上偶然遇到的那些叫玛丽、威廉、安妮、杰克的路人甲们，也都可以成为英雄。
t_en: I think Cinderborne represents a kind of will: it's not just the chosen ones who can become heroes. Those everyday people you bump into along the way, the Marys, Williams, Annies, and Jacks, they can all become heroes too.

t_ch: 先聊到这吧。
t_en: That's enough chit-chat for now.

t_ch: 好嘞～如果还想了解《破晓战队》，可以再来联系我。
t_en: Sure thing~ If you wanna know more about Daybreak Squadron, just come find me again!

t_ch: 先聊到这吧。
t_en: That's enough chit-chat for now.

t_ch: 好嘞～如果还想了解《破晓战队》，可以再来联系我。
t_en: Sure thing~ If you wanna know more about Daybreak Squadron, just come find me again!

t_ch: 您好，星穹列车的贵客。我是二相乐园首席执行官真珠女士的秘书，美亚。
t_en: Hello, esteemed guests of the Astral Express. I am Mia, secretary to Madam Pearl, CEO of Planarcadia.

t_ch: 原则上来说，与真珠女士面谈需要提前预约。但真珠女士嘱咐过，对于贵方成员的会面请求，她会做到随时响应。
t_en: Normally, meetings with Madam Pearl require an appointment in advance. However, she's given strict instructions that whenever your crew requests a meeting, she'll make herself available right away.

t_ch: 尽管如此，但真珠女士近期实在操劳，我以个人名义请求您，如果没有要事相商，请不要过多进出她的办公室。
t_en: Nevertheless, Madam Pearl has been working herself ragged lately. I'm asking you this as a personal favor: unless you have urgent business, please avoid frequenting her office too much.

t_ch: 请问您今天有什么事吗？不妨先和我谈谈，如果我能解决，也就不必打扰真珠女士了。
t_en: Is there something I can help you with today? Why don't you talk to me first? If I can handle it, there's no need to bother Madam Pearl.

t_ch: 有什么需要我帮助的吗？不妨先和我谈谈，如果我能解决，也就不必打扰真珠女士了。
t_en: Is there something I can help you with? Why don't you talk to me first? If I can handle it, there's no need to bother Madam Pearl.

t_ch: 想问你点事情。
t_en: I'd like to ask you something.

t_ch: 当然可以。您请。
t_en: Of course. Please ask away.

t_ch: 秘书的日常工作有哪些？
t_en: What are the daily tasks of a secretary?

t_ch: 全银河的秘书应该都做着差不多的工作，无非是为领导协调行程、传达信息、处理各种琐事。
t_en: Secretaries from all over the cosmos should all do the same thing. They coordinate the schedules for their leaders, convey information, and deal with trivial matters.

t_ch: 这工作说起来不难，但关键在于「善于观察」。
t_en: The job itself isn't hard, but the key is having a sharp eye for detail.

t_ch: 比如从一位客人进门开始，我就是从他的言谈举止中分析这个人的性格涵养，再根据他的约见理由判断事情的轻重缓急。
t_en: For instance, the moment a guest walks through the door, I read their character in their manner and speech, then gauge their request to give it its proper place in the priorities.

t_ch: 必要的时候，用体面的说辞拒绝掉一些「可能带来麻烦」的客人。
t_en: When necessary, I'll need to offer a graceful exit to guests likely to "cause complications."

t_ch: 总之，秘书的工作价值，就是帮首席执行官把精力放在更重要的事情上。
t_en: In short, the whole point of being a secretary is to help the CEO focus on what really matters.

t_ch: 当首席执行官的秘书压力大么？
t_en: Is it stressful being the CEO's secretary?

t_ch: 我做了十几年秘书，之前也服务过其他一些大人物，相比而言，在首席执行官手下工作反而是最轻松的。
t_en: With over a decade of secretarial work under my belt, and having served my fair share of VIPs, I can say this: working for the CEO has been the smoothest ride of all.

t_ch: 职场常言「领导的心思你别猜」，可作为秘书，又必须要揣摩出领导的「弦外之音」，别提有多累人了。
t_en: "Don't try to guess your boss's mind," so goes the workplace wisdom. Yet my entire job as a secretary is to second-guess, to read between the lines. Draining, frankly.

t_ch: 但首席执行官对待下属，怎么说呢…用二相乐园话来说是一个很「直球」的人。
t_en: But how the CEO handles her subordinates... well, to put it in Planarcadia terms, she's what you'd call very "straight-shooting."

t_ch: 她说想换掉办公室的盆栽，那就真的只是想换掉盆栽，而不是暗示你「她不喜欢看到你穿绿色的衬衫来上班」。
t_en: If she says to replace the office plants, she wants the plants replaced, not a hint that "don't you dare show up to work in a green shirt again."

t_ch: 首席执行官是个什么样的人？
t_en: What kind of person is the CEO?

t_ch: 咳咳，「在背后议论领导」可是职场大忌。
t_en: Ahem, "talking about your boss behind their back" is a huge workplace no-no.

t_ch: 不过列车组现在和真珠财团是合作关系，为了让合作更顺畅，我可以分享一些个人看法，以便让您更了解她。
t_en: That said, given the Express Crew and the Pearluxe Corp are in a partnership now, I can offer some personal context to facilitate a smoother collaboration and a clearer understanding of her approach.

t_ch: 首席执行官的工作能力毋庸置疑，大到星际商业谈判，小到日常公文审批，她永远处理得游刃有余。
t_en: There is no doubt about the CEO's executive capacity. From interstellar business deals down to routine document flows, she manages everything with effortless precision.

t_ch: 但她又不是那种没有生活情趣的工作狂，可能你也注意到桌上的绘笔和颜料了。没错，那些都是她的。
t_en: But don't mistake her for a joyless work machine. You probably have noticed the brushes and paints on the desk, right? Yep, those are all hers.

t_ch: 一旦有片刻闲暇，首席执行官就会挥笔作画。在我看来，她的绘画水平丝毫不亚于顶尖绘师。
t_en: Whenever a break can be afforded, the CEO reaches for her brush. Her work, to my eye, rivals that of any top-tier artist out there.

t_ch: 唉，我时常感叹，首席执行官真是个横跨「政、商、艺」三界的奇才啊。
t_en: Time and again, I'm in awe: The CEO is that rare prodigy who commands the mind of a politician, the eye of a merchant, and the hand of an artist.

t_ch: 不打扰你工作了。
t_en: I'll leave you to your work.

t_ch: 哪里，很乐意为您效劳。
t_en: Not at all, happy to be of service.

t_ch: 不打扰你工作了。
t_en: I'll leave you to your work.

t_ch: 哪里，很乐意为您效劳。
t_en: Not at all, happy to be of service.

t_ch: 贵宾您好，欢迎参观珠星本部，需要我为您提供讲解服务么？
t_en: Welcome to Pearluxe Corp HQ, esteemed guest. May I offer my services as your docent?

t_ch: 我想自己逛逛。
t_en: I'd to look around on my own.

t_ch: 没问题，您是贵宾，可以自由参观任何公共区域。
t_en: No problem, all public areas are fully accessible to you as our esteemed guest.

t_ch: 是免费服务么？
t_en: Is this a free service?

t_ch: 当然，您是贵宾，所有参观费用均由珠星本部承担。
t_en: Please be assured, as our esteemed guest, all arrangements for your visit will be covered by Pearluxe Corp HQ.

t_ch: 作为珠星本部的资深介绍员，我还是非常推荐您体验一次讲解服务。
t_en: Speaking as Pearluxe Corp HQ's longtime docent, I highly recommend you give our guided tour a try.

t_ch: 珠星本部的景点众多，我可以根据您的需求制定十几种不同的游览路线，保证您获得最佳的游览体验。
t_en: With the array of attractions at Pearluxe Corp HQ, I can tailor dozens of distinct tour routes to your interests, ensuring your visit is nothing short of exceptional.

t_ch: 可我就喜欢自由探索。
t_en: But I like exploring freely.

t_ch: 不愧是无名客，连游览方式都与常人不同，但「开拓」之道未必在珠星本部适用。
t_en: True to the Nameless, even your way of sightseeing is unique. Yet the ways of the Trailblaze don't always apply here at Pearluxe Corp HQ.

t_ch: 给我一条「快速开宝箱」路线。
t_en: Give me a quick route to hit all the treasure chests.

t_ch: 实在抱歉…这超出了我的能力范围，我会将你的诉求反馈给客服部。
t_en: My sincere apologies... this falls outside my purview. I'll make sure to forward your request to our customer service promptly.

t_ch: 还有一点望贵宾理解，虽然珠星本部的大部分区域都对外开放，但这里毕竟是机铠的研发场所，一些涉及研发机密的「敏感区域」还请止步。
t_en: One more thing, esteemed guests, while most of our HQ is open to the public, this is still a mechatron R&D facility. We ask that you kindly refrain from entering any "restricted domains" that involve proprietary research.

t_ch: 最后祝您在珠星本部玩得愉快，有任何需要随时找我。
t_en: Finally, enjoy your time at Pearluxe Corp HQ, and please don't hesitate to let me know if you require anything.

t_ch: 贵宾您好，是遇到什么问题了么？
t_en: Good day, honored guest. Is there something I can help you with?

t_ch: 确实有些问题想问你。
t_en: There are a few things I'd like to ask you about.

t_ch: 很荣幸为您解答。
t_en: It would be my pleasure to assist you.

t_ch: 为什么用机铠当介绍员？
t_en: Why did they put a mechatron on docent duty?

t_ch: 哈哈，许多游客都会问我这个问题。
t_en: Haha, I get that question from tourists all the time.

t_ch: 按常理说，介绍员这个职业需要给游客亲近感，机铠冷冰冰的形象确实有些违和。
t_en: Common sense would tell you that docents need to come across as warm and approachable, so a cold, rigid mechatron doesn't seem very fitting.

t_ch: 但仔细一想就不难理解，到珠星本部参观的游客，谁不是冲着「机铠诞生地」的名头来的？
t_en: But when you think about it, it makes perfect sense. Why else would anyone tour Pearluxe Corp HQ, if not for the whole "birthplace of mechatrons" fame?

t_ch: 既然如此，就应该在每个环节都最大程度地满足游客的期待，换句话说，机铠介绍员本身也是一处景点。
t_en: Then it only follows that we should deliver on that promise at every turn. In other words, the mechatron docents themselves are part of the attraction.

t_ch: 介绍员的日常工作有哪些？
t_en: What does a docent's daily work involve?

t_ch: 其实介绍员的工作说简单也简单，无非是向游客介绍机铠的发展历史、产品特点、生产流程。
t_en: Actually, when you get down to it, being a docent here boils down to three things: walking visitors through the history, key features, and manufacturing process of the mechatrons.

t_ch: 由于我本人就是机铠，而且已经当了十几年介绍员，这方面的工作早已驾轻就熟。
t_en: Being a mechatron myself, with over a decade of experience, I know this job like the back of my hand.

t_ch: 但是公司最近提升了服务标准，要求介绍员为游客量身定制游览方案。
t_en: But the corp recently upped its service standards, requiring docents to tailor custom itineraries for each visitor.

t_ch: 比如有的游客对愿力着色剂过敏，就要避开「喷涂改造区」；有的游客主要目的是购物，就要多花时间在「商品展览区」。
t_en: For example, tourists with Wishpower coloring paste allergies require itineraries that avoid the "Spray Coating Zone," while those focused on shopping should prioritize time in the "Merchandise Exhibition Area."

t_ch: 游客们的需求五花八门，这份工作也没曾经那样好做了。
t_en: With tourists wanting all sorts of things these days, the once-easy job scope is long gone.

t_ch: 推荐一些景点。
t_en: Got any sightseeing recommendations?

t_ch: 这你算是问对机铠了，我是这里资历最老的介绍员，每个景点玩什么，怎么玩一清二楚。
t_en: Well, you asked the right mechatron! As the most seasoned docent here, I know every attraction inside and out, from the most fun parts to the best routes.

t_ch: 首先必打卡的景点是大厅的「巨型机铠」，它是全乐园最大的机铠，拍照的时候一定记得仰拍，这样才能凸显它的压迫感。
t_en: First stop on the must-see list is the "Giant Mechatron" in the main hall, which is the largest mechatron in the entire arcadia! When you take a picture, remember to shoot from a low angle to really bring out that imposing vibe.

t_ch: 如果您动手能力比较强，那我推荐您在「机铠制造区」亲手打造一台属于自己的机铠，我们有绘师提供现场教学。
t_en: For those with more of a hands-on streak, a unique highlight is the "Mechatron Assembling Zone," where you can assemble your own mechatron with guidance from our in-house artists.

t_ch: 如果您喜欢机铠对战，可以在「机铠竞技场」尽情展示操作，若有幸胜出，还能获得限定款的机铠模型。
t_en: If you're into mechatron duels, the "Mechatron Arena" is your ultimate test. Those who prove themselves in victory will be awarded limited-edition mechatron models.

t_ch: 不过若是您时间充裕，我还是强烈推荐您把所有景点全部体验一遍，绝对不虚此行。
t_en: But if you've got the time, I'd totally recommend hitting up every single attraction. You won't regret it, guaranteed!

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all the questions I have.

t_ch: 那祝您在珠星本部玩得愉快，有任何需要随时找我。
t_en: Well then, enjoy your time at Pearluxe Corp HQ, and please don't hesitate to let me know if you require anything.

t_ch: 没事，你忙吧。
t_en: No worries, you do your thing.

t_ch: 那祝您在珠星本部玩得愉快，有任何需要随时找我。
t_en: Well then, enjoy your time at Pearluxe Corp HQ, and please don't hesitate to let me know if you require anything.

t_ch: 咦，这位{F#小姐姐}{M#小哥哥}，我之前好像没见过你，是新来的吗？
t_en: Oh? Hey there, I don't think I've seen you around before. Are you new here?

t_ch: 不错嘛，看你年纪轻轻的，竟然已经能到珠星财团实习了，将来想必也是前途无量。
t_en: Impressive. To have landed an internship at Pearluxe Corp at this young age, you're clearly destined for great things.

t_ch: 但容我好心提醒你一句：即便再怎么优秀的年轻人，在初入社会时，也都需要经验丰富的前辈加以指导。
t_en: But let me give you a friendly heads-up: Even the brightest young talent could use guidance from a seasoned veteran as they start out in the real world.

t_ch: 不过别担心，今天恰好是你的幸运日——现在站在你眼前的，正是「战略投资部」职级P<unbreak>19</unbreak>的职员——怎么样，要不要交换一下联系方式？
t_en: But don't worry. Lucky for you, you're looking at a P<unbreak>19</unbreak> corporate rank employee of the Strategic Investment Department. So, what do you say? Care to connect?

t_ch: P<unbreak>19</unbreak>…
t_en: P<unbreak>19</unbreak>...

t_ch: …你要指导谁？
t_en: Let me get this straight. Guide who...?

t_ch: 哈哈，不需要我的指导吗？好啊，那让我来考考你：你知道职场生存的七大原则吗？你知道如何轻松背诵每一位领导的生日吗？你知道如何暗算同事吗？
t_en: Haha, think you don't need my guidance? Alright then, let's put you to the test. Do you know the Seven Principles of office politics? Do you know how to commit every executive's birthday to memory? Or how to sabotage a coworker without leaving a trace?

t_ch: 怎么样，被前辈问住了吧？没关系，只要你好好跟我学，这些知识早晚也会融会贯通的。
t_en: See? Got you stumped, didn't I? No worries. Just stick with me, and this stuff will all click into place before you know it.

t_ch: 嗨呀，当然啦，我知道像你这样的「青年才俊」，不是带点身份、就是有些背景。
t_en: Oh, I get it. A "hotshot" like you didn't get here on brains alone. You've got some serious advantages or backing, am I right?

t_ch: 但这珠星财团啊，可不比外面那些小企业，职场的水深着呢…除非你背景硬得像星穹列车那帮无名客，否则要是不懂些人情世故，以后想要往上爬，可就辛苦喽。
t_en: But let me tell you, Pearluxe Corp is another world, nothing like those small-time companies out there... Unless you've got backing as solid as those Nameless folks from the Astral Express, you either know how to play the social game, or climbing that ladder's gonna be one heck of a struggle.

t_ch: 我就是无名客…
t_en: I am a Nameless...

t_ch: 哦！原来如此，是我失敬了…哈哈，得了吧，你要是无名客，那我还是「石心十人」呢。
t_en: Oh! Gotcha, sorry about that... Haha, that's a good one. If you're a Nameless, I'm one of the Ten Stonehearts.

t_ch: 你不认识我？
t_en: You don't know who I am?

t_ch: 哦？难道你很出名吗…没关系，现在我认识你了。
t_en: Oh? Are you famous or something...? Well, doesn't matter. I know you now.

t_ch: 无意自夸，但前辈我啊，可是曾经全凭自己的本事，成功通过二十轮面试，从几千个竞争者中博得头筹呢……
t_en: Not to toot my own horn, but yours truly here managed to ace twenty rounds of interviews and beat out thousands of competitors... all on my own merit, mind you.

t_ch: 更何况我已经在这里工作了五年，经验不可谓不丰富，甚至还曾经有幸在砂金总监手下做过事。
t_en: Not to mention, I've been here for five years, which means my experience is nothing to scoff at. I've even had the honor of working under Director Aventurine himself.

t_ch: 先说好啊，我只是看你合眼缘，起了爱才之心。要是换了别人，主动来找我帮忙，我都要考虑一下呢。
t_en: Just so we're clear, I'm only offering because I like the cut of your jib and can see real talent in you. If it were anyone else coming to me for help, I'd think twice about it, believe me.

t_ch: 怎么样，{F#小姐姐}{M#小哥哥}，现在想要我的联系方式了吗？
t_en: Well now, have I convinced you? Ready to get my info?

t_ch: 哦，特意来找我聊天，是有什么想要向前辈请教的吗？
t_en: Oh, you came all the way to chat with me? Looking to pick my brain about something?

t_ch: 其实并不是特意…
t_en: Actually, it was just a coincidence...

t_ch: 哦，「恰好经过」是吧，我懂我懂~说吧，有什么需要我教教你的？
t_en: Oh, "coincidence," huh? Sure, I get it~ So, what do you need me to teach you?

t_ch: 你经常找人搭讪吗？
t_en: Do you hit on people often?

t_ch: 哎哎哎，怎么说话呢你…那哪儿能叫搭讪？我这是在「扩列」，扩充好友列表，懂吗？
t_en: Hey, hey, hey, watch your words there... Calling it "hitting on people" is way off-base. This is "friendly networking," building connections, you know?

t_ch: 我这种行为，旨在拓展交际圈，拉进同事间距离，好营造一种和谐友爱、共同进步的工作氛围！
t_en: What I'm doing here is expanding my social circle, bringing coworkers closer together, and fostering a harmonious, supportive workplace where everyone thrives together!

t_ch: 所以，那什么…你可千万别和上面打小报告，说我工作时间不饱和什么的啊，知道吗？
t_en: So, um... don't go running to the higher-ups tattling about how I'm not working enough hours or whatever, okay?

t_ch: 你总是这么自信吗？
t_en: Are you always this confident?

t_ch: 当然！让前辈来好好教教你，自信是一种成功人士必备的优良的品质……
t_en: Absolutely! Allow me to enlighten you: Confidence is the cornerstone of a winning mindset...

t_ch: 可你五年了还是P<unbreak>19</unbreak>…
t_en: You've been here five years but is still a P<unbreak>19</unbreak>...

t_ch: 就连账账都有P<unbreak>20</unbreak>。
t_en: Even Numby is a P<unbreak>20</unbreak>.

t_ch: 嘿！你个人，真是白长了张好看的脸，情商怎么这么低呢？！像我这种全凭实力打拼的，能和那种关系户比吗？
t_en: Hey! You've got such looks, yet such lack of insight! Are you seriously putting someone who survives on pure merit in the same category as those nepotism hires?

t_ch: 咳…我这叫稳扎稳打！而且能成为珠星财团的员工，我们本来就已经是精英中的精英了，所以最好收起你那不端正的态度！
t_en: Ahem... it's called being strategic! Besides, just making it into Pearluxe Corp already puts us in the top tier. So I suggest you fix that attitude, pronto!

t_ch: 你啊…没事少听八卦，先保证自己实习期过了再操心别的，知道了吗？
t_en: Listen... forget the office gossip. Your only job right now is to survive the internship. Clear?

t_ch: 你是不是没朋友…？
t_en: Do you... not have any friends?

t_ch: 啧，有一位我很尊敬的前辈——愿琥珀王怜悯他的灵魂——曾经说过一句话：职场生存的最高原则，就是「没有朋友、没有敌人、只有猎物！」
t_en: A senior whose memory I hold dear — may the Amber Lord have mercy on his soul — once said, "The ultimate rule of the corporate jungle is this: no friends, no enemies, only prey!"

t_ch: 这话有点耳熟…
t_en: That sounds familiar...

t_ch: 这熟人我好像也认识…
t_en: I think I know who you're talking about...

t_ch: 难道你也认识那位前辈？那你怎么还会问「有没有朋友」这么幼稚的问题？前辈说的话你是一点都不放在心上啊。
t_en: You know him too? Then why are you asking if I have friends? It's like you didn't listen to a single thing I said.

t_ch: 实话告诉你，那位前辈在公司有着非常独特的生态位，他走以后，留下了一小块生态位的真空。
t_en: To be honest, he had a very specific niche at the IPC. Now that he's gone, there's a bit of a vacancy in that ecosystem.

t_ch: 我认为缅怀他的最佳方式，就是想方设法吃下他的鲸落，替代他曾经的生态位。如果他泉下有知，应该也会欣慰的吧！
t_en: I think the best way to honor him is to feast on his "whale fall" and take over the ecological niche he once filled. I'm sure he'd be happy to see someone fill his shoes!

t_ch: 如果你能想通他「猎物论」中的深刻道理，就来跟着我混吧。咱俩好好配合，一起占据他的生态位。在事成之前，我保证不会背叛你。
t_en: If you can wrap your head around his "Prey Theory," come work with me. We'll make a great team and claim his niche for ourselves. I promise I won't betray you... until the job is done, at least.

t_ch: 先聊到这吧。
t_en: That's enough chit-chat for now.

t_ch: 行吧，有什么想要请教的，可以随时来找我。
t_en: Fair enough. If you ever need some pointers, feel free to drop by.

t_ch: 没事，就来打个招呼。
t_en: Nothing much, just dropping by to say hi.

t_ch: 诶呀，别害羞嘛~仰慕我这种可靠的前辈很正常，不用觉得丢脸~
t_en: Oh, don't be shy! It's only natural to look up to a reliable mentor like me. There's no need to feel embarrassed about it~

t_ch: 欸，又见面了！姬子有没有跟你一起过——看来是没有。
t_en: Hey, we meet again! Did Himeko come along with... Ah, I guess not.

t_ch: 她有没有让你带什么话给我——看来是也没有。
t_en: Did she ask you to give me a message? No? I figured as much.

t_ch: 哎，领航员是这样的，前脚刚和公司的高管聊定合作事宜，后脚还得在列车停靠期间从引擎到散热片全检查一遍。
t_en: Yep, that's how navigators are. One minute she's finalizing deals with IPC executives, and the next she's checking every engine part and heat sink while the Express is docked.

t_ch: 能力越大，责任越大嘛。年轻人，忙点也正常，哎，忙点好，忙点好啊。
t_en: With great power comes great responsibility. It's only natural for young people to be busy. Honestly, it's better that way.

t_ch: 谁让我们家姬子从小就天资聪慧、才华横溢、乐于助人、正气凛然、诚实礼貌、处变不惊、人美心善……
t_en: Our Himeko has always been gifted. Smart, talented, helpful, righteous, honest, polite, and kind-hearted, ever since she was a little girl...

t_ch: 这些话你当面对姬子说过吗？
t_en: Have you ever actually said all that to Himeko?

t_ch: 当然！那可是我的宝贝女儿，就是不知道为什么，每次她都会用相当鄙夷的眼神看着我。
t_en: Of course! She's my precious daughter. I just don't know why she always gives me this look of disgust whenever I say it.

t_ch: 哎，孩子从小就这一个缺点，不经夸。
t_en: Poor girl... she just can't take a compliment.

t_ch: 叔叔你是不是太女儿控了？
t_en: Aren't you a bit of a doting father, sir?

t_ch: 怎么会？我们家很严格的。冰淇淋一次不能吃超过五个，漫画书要看完了才能买新的，可以揍鸽川区的混混，但不能骂老师像「陀螺」，至少不该是「旋风陀螺」。
t_en: No way! I was very strict with her. No more than five ice creams at a time, and no new comics until she finished the old ones. She could beat up thugs in the Dovebrook District, but calling her teacher a "spinning top," or a "cyclone spinner," was where I drew the line.

t_ch: 不过姬子一直都挺乖的，除了高中的时候在家里组装火箭引擎，差点把宅子炸上天以外，基本没做过什么出格的事情。
t_en: But Himeko was always such a good girl. Aside from that one time in high school when she built a rocket engine and nearly blew up the house, she never really did anything out of line.

t_ch: 这么想她，去见本人不好吗？
t_en: If you miss her that much, why don't you just go visit her?

t_ch: 幻月游戏开幕、二相乐园变相横生，她有她的责任在身，我不能在这种时候打扰她。
t_en: Between the Phantasmoon Games and all the chaos in Planarcadia, she's got a lot on her plate. I can't go bothering her at a time like this.

t_ch: 我历来是个长期主义者，只要我一直在这里，等到她愿意来见我的那天就好…我很有耐心的。
t_en: I've always played the long game. As long as I'm here, I can wait for the day she's ready to see me... I have all the patience in the world.

t_ch: 年纪轻轻就跟着星穹列车四处旅行，这一路上，一定吃了不少苦头吧……
t_en: Traveling the stars with the Astral Express at such a tender age, you must have endured so much hardship along the way...

t_ch: 来，这是叔叔的一点心意，就当是零花钱了——千万别告诉姬子是我给的。有空了，也给她买点礼物，她会很高兴的。
t_en: Here, take this as a little "allowance" from me — just don't tell Himeko it came from me. Buy her a little gift when you can. It would mean a lot to her.

t_ch: 谢谢你们，一直以来陪在那孩子身边。
t_en: Thank you for staying by her side all this time.

t_ch: 在找什么吗？校长办公室可不是个「开拓」的好地方。等一切尘埃落定了，给你推荐些更有趣的星球。
t_en: Are you looking for something? The dean's office isn't exactly the best spot for "Trailblazing." I'll suggest some much more exciting planets once the dust settles.

t_ch: 如果想找我聊聊，我倒刚好有些时间。
t_en: I have some time to talk if you're interested.

t_ch: 我确实有不少好奇的事。
t_en: There are quite a few things I'm curious about.

t_ch: 以前的姬子是什么样的？
t_en: So, what was Himeko like as a kid?

t_ch: 姬子啊，从小就比别家的孩子懂事、聪明、好学那么一点点。但因为各种缘故吧，姬子和我的关系，一直都有些疏远。
t_en: Well, Himeko was always a bit more sensible and studious than most children. But for one reason or another, she and I have never been very close.

t_ch: 也是，突然出现在你的人生中、却又时常消失无踪的血亲，和陌生人又有什么区别呢？
t_en: It makes sense, I guess. With a blood relative who flits in and out of your life, what's the difference between that and a stranger?

t_ch: 我所能做的，也只有在寥寥无几的、与她相处的时间里，尽可能地给予我所能给她的一切。
t_en: The best I could do was give her everything I had in those rare moments when we were actually together.

t_ch: 但是嘛，也总有例外的时候，只要我讲起宇宙里的故事，姬子就一定会一声不吭地坐到我的跟前，时不时发出咯咯的笑声。
t_en: But there were always exceptions. Whenever I started telling stories about the universe, Himeko would sit right at my feet, quiet as a mouse, letting out a little giggle every so often.

t_ch: 我低下头，看向她的眼睛，那里的光比任何一颗星星都要漂亮。
t_en: When I looked into her eyes, I saw a light more brilliant than any star in the sky.

t_ch: 你平时的工作是怎样的？
t_en: What's your day job like?

t_ch: 实不相瞒，虽然名义上是校长，但这位置也就是个甩手掌柜的虚职，正事儿都是校董事会在做。
t_en: Honestly, I'm the dean in name only, really. The Board of Directors handles all the actual work.

t_ch: 大多数时候，我都在寰宇间奔走，接受不同雇主委托的绘画工作。
t_en: Most of the time, I'm wandering the cosmos, taking on painting commissions for whoever wants to hire me.

t_ch: 文明或许会消亡，但智慧生命在这一过程中展露的勇气、荣耀与抗争精神，却值得被时间铭记。
t_en: Civilizations might die out, but the courage, glory, and defiance that intelligent life shows before its final moments... those are things time should never forget.

t_ch: 所以，我总会不自觉地期待，接下来会见到怎样的风景。
t_en: That's why I can't help but look forward to the next horizon, wondering what I'll see.

t_ch: 那些光怪陆离的世界，与人们的音容笑貌，在将它们最璀璨、美好的片刻描摹下来的瞬间，就连最聒噪的灵魂也能得到片刻安宁。
t_en: Those fantastical worlds, those smiling faces... the moment I capture their most brilliant, beautiful instances on canvas, even the most turbulent soul finds a moment of peace.

t_ch: 最近在做什么？
t_en: What have you been up to lately?

t_ch: 隆介突然一改常态，严肃地打量起你，仿佛在考察你是否值得信任。
t_en: Ryusuke suddenly turns serious. He studies you with a serious gaze, as if weighing whether you're trustworthy enough for a secret.

t_ch: 其实，我在偷偷画《苍天航路绒绒号》的同人漫画，再过几天就能完工了。
t_en: Between you and me, I'm actually working on a fan comic for Fluffy Across the Blue. It'll be finished in just a couple of days.

t_ch: 为什么？
t_en: Why?

t_ch: 我不能容忍，有人在姬子的创作上胜我一筹，我才是姬子的头号粉丝！那位马老师根本没有掌握到姬奇猫的萌点！
t_en: I simply won't stand for anyone having a better creation of Himeko than me! I'm her number one fan! That Blurry_QR_Colt fellow doesn't even understand what makes Himekat cute!

t_ch: 论及对姬子和星穹列车的了解，我要比他懂上一百倍！
t_en: I know a hundred times more about Himeko and the Astral Express than they ever will!

t_ch: 能剧透下内容吗？
t_en: Care to share any spoilers?

t_ch: 就像著名的领航员，法尔肯•阿蒙森那样，绒绒号的小动物们不断遭遇种种挫折、不幸，在灰暗的宇宙间航行。
t_en: Much like the great Navigator Falcon Amundsen, the little animals on The Fluffy face all sorts of hardships and tragedies as they sail through the dark reaches of the universe.

t_ch: 绒绒号如流星般划过夜空，却又永远焕发着光与热，一次次将绝望的世界照亮——绝对会大卖的。
t_en: The Fluffy streaks through the night like a comet, radiating warmth and light to brighten one desperate world after another... It's going to be a massive hit!

t_ch: 笔名想好了吗？
t_en: Have you picked out a pen name yet?

t_ch: 你倒是提醒我了，用真名肯定会被姬子发现，既然原作者叫「模糊二维马」……
t_en: You've got a point. If I use my real name, Himeko will absolutely find out. Since the original author is "Blurry_QR_Colt"...

t_ch: 干脆叫「摩尔蚊」好了。
t_en: I'll go with "Moirésquito_Pattern."

t_ch: 等完本了，请务必做我的第一个读者。
t_en: You have to be my very first reader once it's complete.

t_ch: 你怎么看待幻月游戏？
t_en: What do you think of Phantasmoon Games?

t_ch: 即使是阿哈这样不可捉摸的神，悬置如此多的神力以置办一场游戏，注定有其理由。
t_en: Even for an unpredictable Aeon like Aha, there must be a reason for committing so much power to organize a game like this.

t_ch: 但祂的真实目的，就不是我们可以捉摸的了。
t_en: But THEIR true motives... those aren't for us to ruminate on.

t_ch: 比起给观众逗乐子这样的表象，身为艺术家，我更好奇，最初的幻月，究竟是为何而升起的。
t_en: Forget the spectacle of entertaining the audience. As an artist, I'm far more curious about why the first Phantasmoon was created in the first place.

t_ch: 无论如何，两千年前的幻月所照映的人们，也不会像今天的二相乐园这样，充斥着疯狂和讥嘲吧。
t_en: No matter what, the people living under the Phantasmoon two thousand years ago couldn't have been as consumed by madness and cynicism as the people of Planarcadia are today.

t_ch: 以后再聊吧。
t_en: Let's talk later.

t_ch: 好啊，往后有什么需要的，都可以来找我。
t_en: Sure thing. Feel free to stop by if you ever need anything.

t_ch: 祝你在幻月游戏中拔得头筹。
t_en: Hope you come out on top in the Phantasmoon Games.

t_ch: 您好！我猜…您是来重新体验机铠战斗的？
t_en: Hi there! Let me guess... back for more mechatron combat?

t_ch: 战！
t_en: Fight!

t_ch: 明白了，我们这就将战斗测试沙盒场地清理出来，您可以再次进行体验。
t_en: Got it. We'll clear out the combat test sandpit right away so you can jump back in.

t_ch: 要是能开出去打就好了。
t_en: If only I could take this out for a real spin.

t_ch: 这…还请您体谅下，即使是在幻造遍地跑的二相乐园，把一个能自由活动的巨型机铠带到大街上去，仍然还是有一定风险的。
t_en: Well... please understand that even in Planarcadia, where imagenae roam free, taking a giant, mobile mechatron out into the public is still a bit risky.

t_ch: 不过，我们还在尽可能优化这个沙盒场地的表现，不管是从匹诺康尼请来知名的怪兽替身演员，还是将大楼被破坏时的碎片做得更碎一些…
t_en: That said, we're doing our best to polish the sandpit experience. We've even hired famous monster stunt actors from Penacony and made the building debris more "shattery" for realism!

t_ch: 毕竟，许多孩子们和成…年纪稍大一点的孩子们，为了体验机铠战斗，会特意办理珠星总部的参观年卡，这对我们来说可是一笔不小的收入。
t_en: After all, plenty of kids and "older" kids buy annual passes to Pearluxe Tower just for the mechatron combat. It's a huge part of our revenue!

t_ch: 当然，您不需要考虑费用问题，根据真珠女士的指示，您可以随时借用模拟场地。祝您体验愉快！
t_en: Also, you don't have to worry about the bill. Per Madam Pearl's orders, the simulation area is yours to use whenever you like. Enjoy!

t_ch: 没事，我只是路过。
t_en: Don't mind me. I'm just passing by.

t_ch: 好的，欢迎您再来。
t_en: Okay then. We look forward to your next visit.

t_ch: 是您啊，想再来体验一次拼装机铠的流程吗？
t_en: Hey there! Back for another round of mechatron assembly?

t_ch: 让我再拼一次。
t_en: I want to build another one.

t_ch: 了解，我们准备了新的体验用板件，这边请！
t_en: You got it. We've got a fresh set of parts ready for you. Right this way!

t_ch: 拼装过程有实际意义吗？
t_en: Is there actually a point to the assembly process?

t_ch: 当然，即使相同型号的机铠，基于拼装流程，也会出现性能差异。
t_en: Definitely. Even with the same mechatron model, the final performance can vary depending on how well it was put together.

t_ch: 这些差异主要体现在「各组件交界区的牢固程度」上——简单来说，要看「交牢」的水平。
t_en: The main difference lies in the "sturdiness of the joints." Basically, it all comes down to your "fitting" skills.

t_ch: 虽说近年来拼装被简化为自动化流程，但诸如装卸时机等差异性仍然存在。只要拼装者坚持不懈地进行体验，交牢水平仍会得以提升。
t_en: Assembly might be mostly automated these days, but things like installation timing still matter. If you keep at it, your "fitting" skills will keep getting better.

t_ch: 所以在机铠对战圈子里，流传着一个简单的道理——「爱拼才会赢」。祝您体验愉快。
t_en: That's why mechatron pilots always say: "Success comes to those who piece things together!" Have a great time!

t_ch: 没事，我只是路过。
t_en: Don't mind me. I'm just passing by.

t_ch: 好的，有需要再叫我。
t_en: Sure thing. Just let me know if you need anything else.

t_ch: 您好，您是{NICKNAME}大人吧，我是负责这片幽囚狱的司狱赤衣。判官雪衣大人已经向我打过招呼了——她说为了查案需要，您可以自由出入幽囚狱的大部分区域。
t_en: Greetings, you must be {NICKNAME}. I am Chiyi, the Jailer overseeing this unit of the Shackling Prison. Madam Xueyi has informed me that you're here to investigate a case. You are free to enter most areas of the Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 不过，我仍然需要提醒您，幽囚狱是罗浮仙舟囚禁重案犯人的所在地，{NICKNAME}大人需要对幽囚狱内所见所闻之事按照保密章程进行守密，请您务必遵守。
t_en: However, I am dutifully obliged to remind you that the Shackling Prison is where the Xianzhou Luofu imprisons our most dangerous felons. {NICKNAME}, {M#sir}{F#madam}, we seek your cooperation to keep everything you see and hear in the Shackling Prison to yourself, as laid out in our confidentiality regulations.

t_ch: 按道理来说我不应该进来？
t_en: So I'm not supposed to be allowed in here then?

t_ch: 按照十王司规定，想要进入幽囚狱的人员都必须经过严格审查，这也是为了保证幽囚狱正常收押工作。大人既然是为查案而来，必然是得到了许可。
t_en: The Ten-Lords Commission imposes a mandatory and stringent assessment on those who wish to enter the Shackling Prison, so that all remand and detention orders within the facility can be performed without disruptions. Since you're here for an investigation, you're naturally granted the relevant permissions.

t_ch: 我听雪衣大人说您是值得信任的仙舟盟友，很少听到那位大人的如此评价，我愿意相信您会将幽囚狱的秘密藏在心中。
t_en: Madam Xueyi has mentioned that you are a trusted ally of the Xianzhou. It is rare to hear her offering such a high remark about anyone, so I am happy to trust that you'll keep mum about the secrets of the Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 要是我不小心说出去的话…？
t_en: And if I unintentionally tell them to someone...?

t_ch: 幽囚狱正常运行上百年，却遭贼人攻破，其中必然是有人泄露幽囚狱相关结构与路线，导致了大量冥差因公殉职，大人应该知道泄密的严重性。
t_en: The Shackling Prison has been operating securely for centuries. If intruders were to infiltrate out of nowhere, it would mean that someone has divulged details of the structural floor plan and routes of the Shackling Prison. Many spiritfarers will end up losing their lives during such an infiltration. I trust you are aware of the grave consequences that come with leaking secrets.

t_ch: 我听雪衣大人说您是值得信任的仙舟盟友，很少听到那位大人的如此评价，我愿意相信您会将幽囚狱的秘密藏在心中。
t_en: Madam Xueyi has mentioned that you are a trusted ally of the Xianzhou. It is rare to hear her offering such a high remark about anyone, so I am happy to trust that you'll keep mum about the secrets of the Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 我自然是相信{NICKNAME}大人的，不过我的工作习惯促使我要再次提醒您「幽囚狱并非是一个来去自由的场所」，大人切要小心行事。
t_en: I am more than happy to put my trust in you, {NICKNAME}. But it is my professional obligation to remind you, once again, that the Shackling Prison is not a place where people walk about freely. Please be cautious in your investigation here.

t_ch: 幽囚狱环境错综复杂，所收押犯人也是三教九流，若{NICKNAME}大人在这里遇到任何问题，都可以随时找我。{NICKNAME}大人如果没有别的问题，现在我就接着忙去了。
t_en: The Shackling Prison has a convoluted layout, and we detain prisoners of various factions here. Feel free to come to me anytime if you need help with something. If you have no other questions, I'll leave you to it, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我现在就有些问题...
t_en: I have some questions for you...

t_ch: 大人您请问。
t_en: Please go ahead and ask your question.

t_ch: 我想了解幽囚狱的历史。
t_en: I want to learn about the Shackling Prison's history.

t_ch: 我并非是研究历史学者，所以我对幽囚狱的历史并非全然熟悉。这里是罗浮内幽囚狱系统中其中一处，历史悠久，建立的具体年份已不可查。
t_en: I am no research historian, so I may not be familiar with the complete history of the Shackling Prison. It has been established eons ago that the exact date of its construction is long-lost to time. The facility we are at is just one unit of the Shackling Prison system on the Luofu.

t_ch: 根据前任司狱大人所说，此处的幽囚狱是由仙舟人和持明族人合作修建的。想来也合理，若不是持明云吟法术，也不可能在海底建造这么庞大的建筑。
t_en: According to the previous Jailer, this unit of the Shackling Prison was jointly built by Xianzhou natives and the Vidyadhara. And it checks out because if not for the Vidyadhara's cloudhymn magic, it wouldn't be possible to build this colossal structure underwater.

t_ch: 现如今，持明龙师也会派遣持明族中擅长云吟法术的青年参与冥差工作，协助维持幽囚狱的水障结构…十王司幽囚狱会永远感谢持明族的付出。
t_en: Even today, the Vidyadhara Preceptors would send Vidyadhara youths skilled in cloudhymn magic to join the spiritfarers in maintaining the prison's flood barriers... The Ten-Lords Commission and The Shackling Prison are eternally grateful for the Vidyadhara's contribution.

t_ch: 我想了解你的工作。
t_en: I want to know more about what you do.

t_ch: 简而言之，就是确保幽囚狱内的犯人待在监禁区域。一旦有犯人敢越雷池半步，我会动用一切手段让他回去。
t_en: To put things simply, my duty is to ensure all prisoners in The Shackling Prison remain within the prison area. If any one of them were to take even half a step beyond the boundaries of the prison, I am expected to employ any possible measure to force them right back.

t_ch: 狱卒们时时刻刻都在提防着这帮重案要犯，越是与他们打交道，我越是感觉到心力交瘁——他们想要离开这里的欲念就像烙铁一般灼热。
t_en: The prison guards have to perpetually keep their guards up around these serious felons. The more I interact with them, the more it grinds down on my mentality — Their desire to leave this facility burns as blazing hot as fiery brands.

t_ch: 司狱大人可以不理会他们？
t_en: But you're the Jailer. Can't you ignore them?

t_ch: 曾经的我也天真过，以为自己可以完全不理会他们就把工作进行下去。
t_en: I used to harbor that naive thought too, believing that I could just block them out and go about my duties.

t_ch: 我必须要从他们试图掩盖的一切的话语和行为中觉察到他们的意图，与此同时，有一些智商极高思维敏捷的犯人也会利用这点来审视我。
t_en: But I need to be able to pick up on their intentions through their words and behavior, which they employ to shield their true thoughts. And while I'm doing that, quick-witted and intelligent prisoners would also use that fact to study me.

t_ch: 我并不擅长与人在智慧上交锋，那种感觉就像是躺在案板上任由持着砍刀的屠夫随意宰割…相信我，你不会喜欢那种感觉的。
t_en: I'm not good at battles of wits. Those make me feel like I'm on a chopping board waiting to be butchered by whoever has the knife... Trust me, it's not a nice feeling to have.

t_ch: 幽囚狱中关了太多犯人，不论道德水准，他们的才华和能力都是顶尖的——这也是他们只能被关在幽囚狱的原因。
t_en: There are too many prisoners locked away in The Shackling Prison. Morality aside, these people are the cream of the crop in terms of talent and capacities — This is precisely why they have to be locked away in The Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 有没有什么奇葩的犯人？
t_en: Are there any particularly strange prisoners?

t_ch: 曾有个身犯多起谋杀案的化外民被押送了进来，根据档案记录，他被称为「勺子」，曾用一柄铁勺子持之以恒地对受害人施害。
t_en: There was an outworlder who got sent in for committing multiple murders. According to the records, he was known as "The Spoon" because he once used an iron spoon to persistently cause harm to the victim.

t_ch: 我原本设想这样的囚犯在单独的囚室之中应该不会有事，后来不知道他从哪里弄了一柄铁勺，持续不断敲击着囚室墙壁，最终让隔壁囚室囚犯精神崩溃了。
t_en: I had assumed he wouldn't cause any trouble if he was locked away in a solitary cell. Yet he somehow managed to get his hands on an iron spoon that he endlessly tapped the wall of his cell with, eventually driving the prisoner in his neighboring cell into a mental breakdown.

t_ch: 这个过程持续了数十年，我低估了这样若有若无的声音对一个人的影响。从此以后，我也算是积累了一些经验……
t_en: The tapping lasted for decades. I shouldn't have underestimated the damage such faintly perceptible noises could do to a person's psyche. I suppose you can say that I gained some experience after that...

t_ch: 我想问的差不多了。
t_en: That's all I wanted to ask.

t_ch: 好的，还有问题可以随时来找我。
t_en: Sure, come look for me anytime if you have more questions.

t_ch: 那你去忙吧。
t_en: I'll leave you to work then.

t_ch: 好的，还有问题可以随时来找我。
t_en: Sure, come look for me anytime if you have more questions.

t_ch: 有些鸟儿终究是关不住的，它们生来自由…不过，关不住的鸟，就只好用弓箭对付了。
t_en: Some birds are never meant to be locked up in cages. They were born to be free... But those that cannot be locked up will have to be shot down with a bow and arrow.

t_ch: 幽囚狱环境错综复杂，所收押犯人也是三教九流，若{NICKNAME}大人在这里遇到任何问题，都可以随时找我。
t_en: The Shackling Prison is a complicated area, and we detain prisoners of all sorts here. Feel free to come to me anytime if you need help with something, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我现在就有些问题...
t_en: I have some questions for you...

t_ch: 大人您请问。
t_en: Please go ahead and ask your question.

t_ch: 我想了解幽囚狱的历史。
t_en: I want to learn about the Shackling Prison's history.

t_ch: 我并非是研究历史学者，所以我对幽囚狱的历史并非全然熟悉。这里是罗浮上幽囚狱系统中其中一处，历史悠久，建立的具体年份已不可查。
t_en: I am no research historian, so I may not be familiar with the complete history of The Shackling Prison. It has been established eons ago that the exact date of its construction is long-lost to time. The facility we are at is just one of the many units of The Shackling Prison system on the Luofu.

t_ch: 根据前任司狱大人所说，此处的幽囚狱是由仙舟人和持明族人合作修建的。想来也合理，若不是持明云吟法术，也不可能在海底建造这么庞大的建筑。
t_en: According to the previous Jailer, this unit of the Shackling Prison was jointly built by Xianzhou natives and the Vidyadhara. And it checks out because if not for the Vidyadhara's cloudhymn magic, it wouldn't be possible to build this colossal structure underwater.

t_ch: 现如今，持明龙师也会派遣持明族中擅长云吟法术的青年参与冥差工作，协助维持幽囚狱的水障结构…十王司幽囚狱会永远感谢持明族的付出。
t_en: Even today, the Vidyadhara Preceptors would send Vidyadhara youths skilled in cloudhymn magic to join the spiritfarers in maintaining the prison's flood barriers... The Ten-Lords Commission and The Shackling Prison are eternally grateful for the Vidyadhara's contribution.

t_ch: 我想了解你的工作。
t_en: I want to know more about what you do.

t_ch: 简而言之，就是确保幽囚狱内的犯人待在监禁区域。一旦有犯人敢越雷池半步，我会动用一切手段让他回去。
t_en: To put things simply, my duty is to ensure all prisoners in The Shackling Prison remain within the prison area. If any one of them were to take even half a step beyond the boundaries of the prison, I am expected to employ any possible measure to force them right back.

t_ch: 狱卒们时时刻刻都在提防着这帮重案要犯，越是与他们打交道，我越是感觉到心力交瘁——他们想要离开这里的欲念就像烙铁一般灼热。
t_en: The prison guards have to perpetually keep their guards up around these serious felons. The more I interact with them, the more it grinds down on my mentality — Their desire to leave this facility burns as blazing hot as fiery brands.

t_ch: 司狱大人可以不理会他们？
t_en: But you're the Jailer. Can't you ignore them?

t_ch: 曾经的我也天真过，以为自己可以完全不理会他们就把工作进行下去。
t_en: I used to harbor that naive thought too, believing that I could just block them out and go about my duties.

t_ch: 我必须要从他们试图掩盖的一切的话语和行为中觉察到他们的意图，与此同时，有一些智商极高思维敏捷的犯人也会利用这点来审视我。
t_en: But I need to be able to pick up on their intentions through their words and behavior, which they employ to shield their true thoughts. And while I'm doing that, quick-witted and intelligent prisoners would also use that fact to study me.

t_ch: 我并不擅长与人在智慧上交锋，那种感觉就像是躺在案板上任由持着砍刀的屠夫随意宰割…相信我，你不会喜欢那种感觉的。
t_en: I'm not good at battles of wits. Those make me feel like I'm on a chopping board waiting to be butchered by whoever has the knife... Trust me, it's not a nice feeling to have.

t_ch: 幽囚狱中关了太多犯人，不论道德水准，他们的才华和能力都是顶尖的——这也是他们只能被关在幽囚狱的原因。
t_en: There are too many prisoners locked away in The Shackling Prison. Morality aside, these people are the cream of the crop in terms of talent and capacities — This is precisely why they have to be locked away in The Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 有没有什么奇葩的犯人？
t_en: Are there any particularly strange prisoners?

t_ch: 曾有个身犯多起谋杀案的化外民被押送了进来，根据档案记录，他被称为「勺子杀人狂」，曾用一柄铁勺子持之以恒地对受害人施害。
t_en: There was an outworlder who got sent in for committing multiple murders. According to the records, he was known as "The Spoon Murderer" because he once used an iron spoon to persistently cause harm to the victim.

t_ch: 我原本设想这样的囚犯在单独的囚室之中应该不会有事，后来不知道他从哪里弄了一柄铁勺，持续不断敲击着囚室墙壁，最终让隔壁囚室囚犯精神崩溃了。
t_en: I had assumed he wouldn't cause any trouble if he was locked away in a solitary cell. Yet he somehow managed to get his hands on an iron spoon that he endlessly tapped the wall of his cell with, eventually driving the prisoner in his neighboring cell into a mental breakdown.

t_ch: 这个过程持续了数十年，我低估了这样若有若无的声音对一个人的影响。从此以后，我也算是积累了一些经验……
t_en: The tapping lasted for decades. I shouldn't have underestimated the damage such faintly perceptible noises could do to a person's psyche. I suppose you can say that I gained some experience after that...

t_ch: 我想问的差不多了。
t_en: That's all I wanted to ask.

t_ch: 好的，还有问题可以随时来找我。
t_en: Sure, come look for me anytime if you have more questions.

t_ch: 那你去忙吧。
t_en: I'll leave you to work then.

t_ch: 好的，还有问题可以随时来找我。
t_en: Sure, come look for me anytime if you have more questions.

t_ch: 原来是仙舟外远道而来的老板啊，今天是要来算一卦吗？
t_en: Ah, an esteemed customer visiting Xianzhou from afar. Are you here for a divination today?

t_ch: 我见老板您是第一次来，请允许我介绍一下自己——前太卜司天才卜者，现鉴爻轩小掌柜，若月是也。
t_en: Since it's your first time here, allow me to introduce myself. I am the genius diviner, formerly of the Divination Commission, now a humble shopkeeper of the Looking Bronze. My name is Ruoyue.

t_ch: 老板您无论是打算择吉避凶、还是想查个生辰八字、或者卜个姻缘、甚至只是求个名字，小店都将竭诚为您服务。
t_en: Whether you're seeking fortune or safety, or simply wish to check your horoscope, to find love, or even to request a name for a child, I am humbly at your service.

t_ch: 我想给自己取个名。
t_en: I wish to name myself.

t_ch: 老板您是要给自己取名字吗？哎呀，这样的需求我还是见的比较少。
t_en: Oh, seeking a name for yourself? That is a rare request indeed.

t_ch: 看着客官的面相，我再掐指一算，心中已经有了一个好名字了。
t_en: Looking at you, and with a pinch of my fingers, and I've got the perfect name for you.

t_ch: 您看「二狗」如何？
t_en: How about "Noob"?

t_ch: 还不如{NICKNAME}呢。
t_en: That's worse than {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 没毛病啊老板，您这名字已经取得足够好了，我看倒也不必更改。
t_en: That sounds just fine. Your name is fine as it is. I see no need to change it.

t_ch: 这其实就是我的名字。
t_en: That IS my name.

t_ch: 没想到您仪表堂堂，还有着如此震耳威名。
t_en: I wouldn't have known that one with such elegant flair as yours would have such a resounding name.

t_ch: 老板您有所不知，名字是最短的咒语，如果您遭遇危急关头，请大声叫出你的名字——二狗，定会对你有所帮助。
t_en: You may not be aware, but a name is the shortest form of a spell. If you're ever in peril, just yell out your name "Noob," and it will surely be useful to you.
